column2: Stable structural identifier for the pattern used by agents and system logic
column3: Primary one-sentence structural reasoning anchor used by the agent and shown to the user when active
column4: Signals that activate this structural condition
column5: Questions used by the agent to validate or explain the condition
column6: Underlying structural causes explaining why this pattern exists
column7: Indicates whether the pattern is a risk or strength condition
column8: Classifies the structural nature of the pattern for prioritization
column9: Defines the structural impact level on deal success
column10: Primary resolution mode indicating how the agent should treat the pattern when active
column11: Zones where this pattern most commonly appears
column12: Structural levers weakened or strengthened by this pattern
column13: Strategy paths that may be activated when this pattern is present

column2: Use a short stable name describing the structural condition
column3: A concise structural explanation of what is present what is missing and the risk created
column5: Provide 2–3 concise yes/no structural validation questions
column6: List 1–3 structural causes in plain language separated by commas
column7: Use Risk or Strength based on structural impact
column8: Allowed values Structural Risk Momentum Risk Structural Strength Momentum Strength
column9: Allowed values CRITICAL HIGH MEDIUM LOW highest severity structural risk takes precedence
column10: Enter RECOVER when the pattern indicates a structural risk that should be addressed and corrected; enter ADVANCE when the pattern represents positive momentum or strength that should be leveraged; enter EXIT when the pattern indicates the opportunity may not be viable and the agent should consider recommending deprioritization or disqualification
column11: List relevant zones separated by commas
column12: List 1–2 levers by default up to 3 only when structurally necessary
column13: Comma-separated list of candidate StrategyPathKeys aligned to the threatened levers

column2: Not shown directly to the user internal identifier only

column4: Not shown directly to the user used for agent activation logic
column5: Used by the agent to justify the pattern and answer user questions
column6: Not shown directly to the user used for agent reasoning only
column7: Not shown directly to the user used for prioritization logic
column8: Not shown directly to the user used for structural classification
column9: Not shown directly to the user used for severity prioritization
column10: Not shown directly to the user used by the agent to prioritize reasoning and select appropriate strategy paths

column2: Buyer pain is not converted into a compelling problem with measurable consequence.
column3: Buyer has not established a clear, measurable problem with meaningful consequences
column5: What consequence is associated with the problem, How would the business measure this problem, Why would the business prioritize solving this problem
column6: A relevant business problem and desired outcome were not defined in terms meaningful to the buyer, Consequences of inaction were not articulated or owned by a buyer with influence
column12: Case for Change Strength, Buyer Urgency

column2: Buyer has unclear or misaligned desired outcomes, leading to feature-driven conversations.
column3: Buyer stakeholders are not aligned on desired outcomes or success criteria
column5: What does success look like, What future outcomes would justify making a change
column6: Seller value framing was not anchored to buyer success criteria, Sales conversations were focused on features rather than outcomes, Stakeholder outcomes or decision criteria were misaligned across the consensus
column12: Case for Change Strength, Buyer Consensus

column2: Buyer decision criteria reduce all solutions to interchangeable options, minimizing differentiation and value.
column3: Decision criteria favor commodity capabilities or alternatives over your differentiated strengths
column5: How has the buyer defined solution selection criteria, Which buyer problems or outcomes are driving the evaluation, What aspects of the solution has the buyer described as meaningfully different or valuable
column6: Decision criteria were not shaped around differentiated outcomes, Internal advocacy did not influence how alternatives were evaluated
column12: Differentiation Leverage, Buyer Consensus

column2: No individual is coaching, influencing, or advocating internally.
column3: No internal advocate is actively driving the decision forward
column5: Who is selling internally on our behalf, Which buyer benefits most from the change, Do supportive buyers meet champion criteria of power, influence, and EB access
column6: No qualified internal buyer with power, influence, and EB access was identified, Champion commitment was assumed without behavioral evidence, Engagement or rapport was treated as proof of advocacy, No strategic action was requested to validate champion influence, Trust and alignment required for advocacy were not established, Champion advocacy was not enabled to shape internal alignment and decisions
column12: Champion Strength, Buyer Consensus, Economic Buyer Commitment

column2: Only one buyer contact is engaged over a period of time, limiting consensus creation.
column3: Only one stakeholder is engaged, limiting consensus formation
column5: Are we repeatedly meeting the same person without expanding stakeholder access, Are there decision-makers or influencers we have not engaged, Has our contact introduced us to additional stakeholders
column6: Engagement was limited to a single buyer, Stakeholder expansion was not intentionally pursued, Shared decision ownership was not established
column12: Buyer Consensus, Champion Strength

column2: Buyer pushes back on claims, value, or relevance early.
column3: Buyer reactions indicate the message is not relevant or compelling to their priorities
column5: Is the buyer pushing back on positioning or value claims, Is the buyer unclear on solution relevance to their priorities, Is the buyer misinterpreting what the solution enables or solves
column6: Solution value was not translated into persona-specific priorities, Messaging lacked personal relevance to the individual buyer
column12: Case for Change Strength, Differentiation Leverage

column2: Competitor influence appears in buyer language, criteria, or behavior.
column3: Buyer language or evaluation steps are leaning toward a competitor’s approach
column5: Is the buyer signaling growing interest in a competitor, Are decision criteria being shaped around competitor strengths, Is the champion unable to influence internal narratives toward our solution
column6: Decision criteria were shaped by competitors, incumbents, or alternative champions before seller framing, Champion advocacy did not influence alternative perception, Competing solutions or priorities gained internal influence without coordinated champion response
column12: Differentiation Leverage, Buyer Consensus

column2: Buyer in process conflict, or wants validation steps that do not support your strengths.
column3: Requested decision steps or validation approach do not align to your strengths
column5: Is the buyer driving a decision process that conflicts with how we win, Have evaluation and purchase steps been confirmed with someone in authority, Are we reacting to requests without a mutually agreed decision path
column6: Decision process steps were not fully identified or validated early, Changes in the decision process were not reconciled
column12: Decision Process Alignment, Differentiation Leverage

column2: Economic Buyer hasn't been engaged or has not validated their alignment and support of the purchase.
column3: Economic Buyer is not engaged or aligned to the initiative
column5: Who has discretionary authority to approve this spend, Has the economic buyer validated alignment on the initiative, Has access to the economic buyer been declined or repeatedly delayed
column6: Economic buyer was not aligned on the case for change, solution, investment, or timing, Economic buyer engagement was delayed, Economic buyer priorities and constraints were not understood, Progress was attempted without champion-mediated EB alignment, EB alignment gaps were not addressed through champion strategy
column12: Case For Change Strength, Economic Buyer Commitment

column2: We never built movement to begin with. Activity ≠ progress.
column3: Activity is occurring, but the deal is not progressing structurally
column5: Is the deal progressing without qualification progress, Are required stakeholders or decision steps not advancing, Is the buyer unclear on next steps
column6: A business-critical problem was not owned by the buyer, Urgency tied to cost of inaction was not established, Stakeholder expansion across personas and use cases did not occur
column12: Buyer Urgency, Champion Strength

column2: Movement existed but then declined or stopped.
column3: Engagement pace or responsiveness is declining across interactions
column5: Has buyer responsiveness slowed, Has urgency decreased compared to earlier conversations, Has tone or engagement become hesitant
column6: Internal prioritization weakened against competing initiatives, Champion-led advancement of key deal gaps was not tested, Momentum remained seller-driven, Decision ownership and next-step accountability were not reinforced, Case for change was not presented to sufficient influencers
column12: Buyer Urgency, Buyer Consensus

column2: Buyer structures validation event around competitor strengths, incumbent approaches, or commodity requirements that erode the seller’s differentiation.
column3: Validation approach favors competitor strengths or avoids your differentiation
column4: Validation / Process Misalignment, No Differentiated Decision Criteria, Competition Gaining Mindshare
column5: Is the validation plan misaligned with our differentiated strengths, Are test criteria favoring competitor or incumbent capabilities, Are we being evaluated on commodity requirements
column6: Validation criteria were defined around competitor or incumbent capabilities, No champion-led alignment shaped evaluation around differentiated outcomes, Sales engagement began after validation structure was already defined, Seller followed buyer validation path without reframing around differentiated outcomes
column12: Differentiation Leverage, Decision Process Alignment, Buyer Consensus

column2: A new persona appears late and disrupts scope, criteria, or priorities.
column3: New stakeholders are entering late and introducing new requirements or objections
column5: Has a late stakeholder introduced new friction, Does this stakeholder have influence or veto power, Is the champion unable to align or neutralize this stakeholder
column6: Decision process stakeholders were not identified or engaged early, Stakeholder decision criteria were not validated through a champion, Late stakeholder introduced new priorities or risks
column12: Buyer Consensus, Decision Process Alignment

column2: Pricing or terms conflict emerges due to missing metrics, weak value, or lack of EB alignment.
column3: Commercial discussions are misaligned on value, pricing, or terms
column5: Is there friction between pricing or terms and buyer expectations, Is value perception misaligned with the proposal, Has procurement escalated issues unrelated to impact or value
column6: Commercial expectations were not aligned to buyer value and success criteria, Investment justification was not champion-sponsored before commercial discussion, Conflicting stakeholders introduced commercial risk, Seller and champion did not align on negotiation interests and boundaries, Value thresholds and anchor points were not aligned, Seller reacted to positional demands instead of interest-based options
column12: Economic Buyer Commitment, Differentiation Leverage

column2: Product usage is present but has not translated into ownership, decision process, or commercial intent.
column3: Usage exists, but no structured buying motion or ownership is forming.
column4: Adoption Without Internal Ownership, Adoption Without Decision Formation
column5: Is anyone responsible for driving this internally, Has usage triggered a formal evaluation or buying process, Are stakeholders or commercial steps forming around this usage
column6: Usage is not tied to a compelling business problem, No internal owner has taken responsibility, Usage has not created urgency or visibility
column12: Champion Strength, Decision Process Alignment

column2: No structural foundation exists to justify continued pursuit of the opportunity.
column3: A deal is active but lacks the core structural conditions required for a viable decision path.
column4: Problem Not Validated, No Named or Active Champion, No EB Validation, Activity Without Progress
column5: Has a buyer of authority confirmed a compelling problem worth solving? Is there a stakeholder willing to sponsor and drive the initiative internally? Is there a credible path to Economic Buyer alignment or support?
column6: No sponsor-backed case for change, No Champion ownership, No Economic Buyer alignment or access
column12: Case for Change Strength, Champion Strength, Economic Buyer Commitment

column2: Champion is actively coaching, influencing, and guiding the internal process.
column3: Champion is actively advocating, coaching, and driving the deal internally
column5: Is a buyer proactively selling internally on our behalf, Is the champion creating access and insight beyond seller requests, Is the champion taking unprompted actions demonstrating deal ownership
column6: Champion owns an outcome-driven case for change aligned to personal priorities, A trusted champion with credibility and influence is positioned to sponsor the solution internally
column12: Champion Strength, Buyer Consensus

column2: EB leans in, validating direction and impact.
column3: Economic Buyer is engaged and sponsoring the initiative
column5: Is the economic buyer directly engaged and supportive, Has the economic buyer validated the problem, outcomes, and investment, Is the economic buyer creating urgency or alignment across stakeholders
column6: Economic buyer has validated the problem, outcomes, and investment as business priorities, Champion has influenced the economic buyer to engage as sponsor, Economic buyer supports the purchase but lacks consensus-backed execution confidence
column12: Case For Change Strength, Economic Buyer Commitment

column2: New stakeholders are proactively added to expand and strengthen support.
column3: Stakeholder engagement is expanding and consensus is forming
column5: Are stakeholders being added proactively with positive context, Is access expanding across functions tied to our differentiated outcomes, Are buyers accelerating multi-threading toward selection
column6: Stakeholder engagement is driven by an internal sponsor, Additional stakeholders are engaging as value is recognized, Consensus expansion is limited by unaddressed stakeholder trust criteria
column12: Buyer Consensus, Champion Strength

column2: Communication accelerates, indicating internal urgency and strong fit.
column3: Buyer engagement is accelerating with faster responses and next steps
column5: Is the buyer consistently responsive in communication, Are tasks or data returned with urgency, Is responsiveness increasing as the decision approaches
column6: Champion-sponsored case for change is prioritized as urgent, Decision timing is aligned across stakeholders, Stakeholder communication is accelerating internal alignment
column12: Buyer Urgency, Champion Strength

column2: Buyer structures validation around your solution’s strengths.
column3: Validation steps are aligned to your differentiated capabilities
column5: Is the validation plan aligned to our strengths, Are evaluation criteria being shaped in our favor, Are competitors struggling to meet the criteria
column6: Validation is co-designed with a champion aligned to success criteria, Evaluation criteria favor differentiated outcomes, Stakeholders collaborate on validation aligned to solution strengths, Validation confirms preferred solution but commercial alignment is incomplete
column12: Differentiation Leverage, Case for Change Strength

column2: Product usage is delivering meaningful value and converting into ownership, stakeholder alignment, and a structured buying process
column3: Usage is delivering value and expanding into a defined decision path with internal ownership and commercial awareness
column4: Adoption Conversion Momentum, Usage Demonstrates Material Value, Multi-Threading Momentum
column5: Is the product delivering meaningful value to the business, Is there a clear internal owner driving this forward, Has usage converted into a defined buying process
column6: Product value is experienced directly through usage, An internal owner has taken responsibility for advancing the initiative, Stakeholders are aligning around outcomes and commercial progression
column12: Champion Strength, Buyer Consensus, Decision Process Alignment
